channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan Tops Global Air Pollution Rankings in 2025
item.description: According to the latest annual report by Swiss air quality firm IQAir, Pakistan was ranked the world's most polluted country in 2025, with PM2.5 levels reaching up to 13 times higher than the safe lim...

item.title: B.C. driver fined for falling asleep at wheel in Tesla during rainy rush hour
item.description: Police in Metro Vancouver issued a warning to drivers after a woman was caught asleep at the wheel of her Tesla on Highway 1 during the morning rush hour in Coquitlam, B.C. The officer observed the dr...

item.title: AREWA24 announces return of hit Hausa drama series Zabi Biyu for Season 2
item.description: Entertainment lovers go get another sweet time as AREWA24, the leading Hausa language entertainment and lifestyle TV channel for Nigeria and West Africa, don announce say their popular drama series 'Z...

item.title: Cuttable reaches $100 million valuation after founder's $2 billion exit from A Cloud Guru
item.description: Sam Kroonenburg, who made headlines after selling his first company, A Cloud Guru, for $2 billion to Pluralsight in 2021, has achieved another significant milestone with his new venture, Cuttable. Fou...

item.title: James Carville Rebukes Bill Maher Over Trump Criticism on Podcast
item.description: Democratic strategist James Carville unleashed a profanity-laced response to comedian Bill Maher on the 'Politics War Room' podcast during a Politicon event. Carville directly addressed Maher's accusa...

item.title: Imran Khan: The struggle against political and media suppression in Pakistan
item.description: Imran Khan, Pakistan's most renowned public figure, has faced extraordinary attempts to erase his presence from national life following his removal as prime minister in 2022. Once celebrated as a cric...

item.title: Why Taking Time to Listen Matters in Journalism
item.description: In many offices, newsrooms, and even government corridors in Tanzania, the most meaningful conversations often start not in boardrooms but over a simple cup of tea. Angel Navuri from Mwananchi Communi...

item.title: Pakistan Records Highest Air Pollution Levels Globally in 2025
item.description: According to the latest report by Swiss air quality monitoring company IQAir, Pakistan emerged as the most polluted country in the world in 2025. The research highlighted that PM2.5 concentrations, wh...

item.title: Universities Should Focus on Guiding Ethical AI Use Rather Than Policing It
item.description: Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) has already made its way into university classrooms, but many institutions still respond with fear and surveillance, warning students against using it. Willie C...

item.title: Commercial Crime in South Africa: Rapid Growth and Rising Concerns
item.description: In South Africa, commercial crime has been steadily increasing over the past decade, emerging as one of the fastest-growing forms of criminal activity. According to recent police statistics, cases hav...

item.title: Royal Mail accused of instructing postal workers to conceal mail to meet delivery targets
item.description: Postal workers across the United Kingdom have alleged that Royal Mail managers instructed them to hide or move undelivered mail to create the impression that delivery targets were being met. According...

item.title: Former Nigerian Footballer Calls for EFCC and ICPC Investigation into NFF FIFA Fund Mismanagement
item.description: Former Nigerian footballer, Harrison Jalla, don call on the EFCC and ICPC make dem investigate Nigerian Football Federation (NFF) over how dem don misuse FIFA grants. Jalla talk say if Congo fit jail ...

item.title: Chancellor Reeves outlines approach to future energy bill support amid international uncertainty
item.description: Chancellor is set to address MPs regarding the domestic impact of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East and its economic implications for the UK. Her speech is expected to cover three main areas: an...

item.title: State Street CIO warns of private credit risks amid geopolitical tensions
item.description: Lori Heinel, the chief investment officer at State Street, which manages around US$6 trillion (A$8.6 trillion), has expressed concerns over a potential 'doom loop' in the private credit sector. Recent...

item.title: Understanding Chemotherapy: Safe Practices for Patients and Caregivers at Home
item.description: When a family member starts chemotherapy, many caregivers panic over what can or cannot be shared at home. Dr Andrew Odhiambo, a consultant medical oncologist at the Nairobi Hospital Cancer Centre, sa...

item.title: Tensions in the Middle East Escalate as Iran Denies US Talks and Energy Crisis Worsens
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have intensified as the US-Israeli conflict with Iran continues. Donald Trump claimed that talks with Iran had produced 'major points of agreement', suggesting a potential ...

item.title: Understanding Love, Friendship, and Uncertainty in Sally Rooney's 'Normal People'
item.description: The book 'Normal People' by Sally Rooney na story ya two young people, Marianne na Connell, wey dem grow together but come from very different social classes. The story shows how human beings dey need...

item.title: How Australia’s experience with gender equality can help Kenya strengthen laws and leadership
item.description: The article looks at how Kenya can learn from Australia’s long‑term journey in promoting gender equality, especially in turning good laws into real change on the ground. It points out that Kenya alrea...

item.title: Former Minister Raphael Tuju Receives Medical Care While in Police Custody at Karen Station
item.description: Former Cabinet Minister Raphael Tuju, a well-known political figure in Kenya, was treated by doctors from Karen Hospital while being held at the Karen Police Station on Monday. He reportedly complaine...

item.title: Kim Jong-un Declares North Korea’s Nuclear Status Permanent and Warns South Korea
item.description: North Korean leader Kim Jong-un stated that North Korea will continue to strengthen its nuclear forces permanently, designating South Korea as its most hostile neighbor. In a speech to the Supreme Peo...
